features.name: Copilot Email Intelligence & Quality
features.description: Use model router for intelligence-tier-based model selection in email generation, add user sign-offs, prohibit em dashes, broaden detection patterns.

stories.title: Fetch and inject Fathom summary into context block
acceptance: buildDealContextBlock includes structured meeting summary (key takeaways, action items, pricing) from top 2 meetings
Summary is extracted from meetings.summary JSON field (Fathom markdown_formatted), condensed to ~400 tokens per meeting
Falls back gracefully when summary is null or malformed
Console debug log shows summary injection

stories.title: Inject deal truth fields into context block
acceptance: Context block includes deal truth fields (pain, champion, economic_buyer, success_metric, next_step, top_risks) when available
Fetched via single query to deal_truth_fields table scoped by deal_id
Fields with confidence < 0.3 are excluded
No extra latency — runs in parallel with existing enrichment queries

stories.title: Enrich greeting with meeting-specific insights
acceptance: Greeting shows last meeting date and one key takeaway (not just oneliner)
If next_step truth field exists, greeting shows it as a proactive nudge
Greeting mentions contact name when resolved

stories.title: Write back resolved contact and company links on enrichment
acceptance: When enrichment resolves a contact_id or company_id that the deal is missing, PATCH deals table with the resolved values
Also writes contact_name and contact_email when resolved
Only writes if the deal field is currently null (never overwrites existing links)
Non-blocking — fire-and-forget PATCH, does not delay enrichment

stories.title: Create batch heal edge function for nightly run
acceptance: Edge function heal-deal-links processes deals with null primary_contact_id or company_id
Uses same resolution logic as enrichment: match contacts by company name/email domain, match companies by name/domain
Processes in batches of 50, updates deals table directly
Logs count of healed links per run

stories.title: Schedule nightly heal job via pg_cron
acceptance: Migration adds pg_cron job calling heal-deal-links edge function daily at 03:00 UTC
Cron job passes service_role auth
Job is scoped to active deals only (status = 'active')

stories.title: Create deal_dossiers table schema
acceptance: deal_dossiers table with: id, deal_id (unique), org_id, snapshot (JSONB), last_meetings_hash, updated_at, created_at
snapshot JSONB structure: { narrative, key_facts[], stakeholders[], commitments[], objections[], timeline[] }
RLS policy: users can read/write dossiers for deals they own or share org with
Index on deal_id for fast lookup

stories.title: Build dossier on copilot session close
acceptance: When copilot chat has >= 2 user messages and the sheet closes, trigger a dossier synthesis
Sends conversation + enrichment to copilot-autonomous with a hidden system message requesting a structured dossier extract
Upserts result into deal_dossiers table (on conflict deal_id)
Non-blocking — runs after sheet close animation

stories.title: Load dossier into context block on copilot open
acceptance: On enrichment fetch, also load deal_dossiers row for this deal
If dossier exists, inject snapshot.narrative + key_facts into the [DEAL_CONTEXT] block
Dossier data appears under a [DEAL_HISTORY] section in the context
Greeting references dossier insights (e.g. 'Last session you discussed pricing')

stories.title: Auto-update dossier when new meetings are synced
acceptance: When a meeting summary completes (summary_status = 'complete'), check if the meeting's contact/company links to a deal with an existing dossier
If yes, append the new meeting's key takeaways to the dossier's timeline and update narrative
Uses a meetings_hash field to avoid reprocessing already-incorporated meetings
Triggered via database webhook or pg_notify on meetings.summary_status change

stories.title: Wire model router into email draft generation
acceptance: Import resolveModel from modelRouter.ts into structureEmailDraftResponse
Call resolveModel with feature='copilot', userId, orgId from context to get the tier-appropriate model
When provider is 'anthropic', call Claude API (same pattern as line 4567) with resolved modelId
Keep Gemini as fallback when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is missing or model router fails
Log resolved model name in console for debugging

stories.title: Add em dash prohibition and sign-off instructions to email prompt
acceptance: Add 'Never use em dashes anywhere in the email' to INSTRUCTIONS block
Extract signoffs array from style_metadata.signoffs and style_metadata.greetings_signoffs.signoffs
Include preferred sign-offs in style instruction with 'USE ONE OF THESE EXACTLY'
Update instruction #7 to reference user's preferred sign-off from writing style
Add wordsToAvoid from style_metadata to prompt

stories.title: Broaden email draft detection patterns
acceptance: Add 'follow up' and 'follow-up' patterns without requiring 'email' keyword (exclude when 'task' present)
Add 'reach out to' and 'get in touch' patterns
Existing detection patterns remain unchanged (no regression)

stories.title: Deploy copilot-autonomous to staging and verify
acceptance: Deploy copilot-autonomous with --no-verify-jwt to staging project caerqjzvuerejfrdtygb
Verify edge function logs show resolved model name (e.g., claude-sonnet-4-6-20250514 for medium tier)

parallelGroups.name: Group 1: Model Router Integration
parallelGroups.note: Foundation - wire model router into email generation

parallelGroups.name: Group 2: Quality Improvements (parallel)
parallelGroups.note: Em dashes + signoffs and detection broadening can run in parallel after INTEL-001

parallelGroups.name: Group 3: Deploy & Verify
parallelGroups.note: Deploy after all code changes complete
